title: Elsholtzia pilosa
intro: Elsholtzia pilosa, also known as "pilose elsholtzia" or "pilose hairy elsholtzia", is a perennial herb native to China, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a traditional remedy for various ailments, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Elsholtzia pilosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Elsholtzia pilosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being dried and used as a herbal powder or added to soups and other dishes.
taxonomy: Elsholtzia pilosa is classified within the family Lamiaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Elsholtzia, which is native to Asia and is commonly found in grasslands and open woodlands. The family Lamiaceae, also known as the mint family, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes many popular herbs and ornamentals. The classification of Elsholtzia pilosa reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Lamiaceae family. Elsholtzia pilosa is also sometimes referred to as Vietnamese mint, due to its native range and culinary uses.
uses: Elsholtzia pilosa is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. It is said to have antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating infections and wounds. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat digestive issues and fever. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
